codeReview.summary: All 3 fixes are correctly implemented with appropriate regression tests. Fix 1 (SECURITY): clojure.edn/read-string properly replaces clojure.core/read-string in pipeline/core.clj — the edn namespace is required with alias and the single call site updated. Fix 2 (PERFORMANCE): noise-groups map is correctly hoisted from inside the mapv callback to a let binding outside it, preserving identical logic while reducing O(n²) to O(n). Fix 3 (CORRECTNESS): clojure.string and clojure.set are explicitly required with aliases str/set in verification/core.clj, and all 5 fully-qualified call sites updated to use aliases. 9 regression tests added covering security scanning, functional correctness, determinism, disjointness, and ns require verification. One non-blocking inconsistency: the test file itself uses clojure.string fully-qualified without requiring it, which is the same fragility pattern Fix 3 addresses.

issues.description: Test uses clojure.string/split-lines, clojure.string/trim, and clojure.string/join (lines 27, 36, 38) with fully-qualified names but does NOT require clojure.string in its ns form. This is ironic given Fix 3 was specifically about eliminating reliance on transitive namespace loading. Works in practice because the test runner loads clojure.string via other namespaces, but contradicts the convention being enforced.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md does not document the security convention of using clojure.edn/read-string instead of clojure.core/read-string for parsing untrusted or file-sourced EDN data. This was the root cause of the security issue in pipeline/core.clj and could recur in future features that read EDN files.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Fix 1 replaced clojure.core/read-string at pipeline/core.clj:196. AGENTS.md 'Coding Conventions' section has no mention of read-string security.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md does not document the convention of always using explicit :require with aliases rather than fully-qualified namespace calls (e.g. clojure.string/join) that depend on transitive loading. This was the root cause of the correctness issue in verification/core.clj and the worker's own test file still violates this pattern.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Fix 3 added explicit :require for clojure.string and clojure.set in verification/core.clj. The test file pipeline_polish_test.clj still uses clojure.string/split-lines without requiring it (line 27).

summary: Feature passes review. All 3 scrutiny-discovered issues (edn/read-string security, noise-groups O(n) performance, explicit ns requires) are correctly fixed with 9 regression tests. One non-blocking issue: the test file itself uses clojure.string without explicit require, contradicting the convention Fix 3 enforces. Two shared-state observations about undocumented conventions (read-string security, explicit requires) that could prevent recurrence.
